blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Laredos is our go-to on Sandy Plains when we want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss. The inside is colorful and festive, and it has that cantina feel that makes it work for a family dinner or a casual night out with friends. The margaritas are a big draw and worth ordering.

They do all the Mexican classics well. It's the kind of place where everyone at the table finds something they want, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for a weeknight when you don't want to cook but also don't want to spend a lot.

They're open every day until at least 10pm, which is handy when the dinner decision happens late.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside is the pizza place people around here keep coming back to, and once you go you'll understand why. It's casual and low-key, good for a family dinner or a Friday night when you just want really good pizza without making a whole production of it.

Start with the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings if they're on the menu. People rave about the sauce and some even save it to dip their crust into. The Caesar Salad is also worth ordering, simple and done right. For the pizza itself, the Hot Honey Char has been getting a lot of love from regulars.

They're closed Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Macaw is a little Brazilian spot on Roswell Road that I didn't know I needed until I tried it. The whole vibe is warm and low-key, and the staff actually make you feel welcome. It's a great stop after school pickup, a workout, or honestly just whenever you want something that feels a little different.

The açaí is self-serve style, so you pile on whatever toppings you want and as much as you like. They also have crepes and coxinha, which is a Brazilian fried chicken snack. My kids love it and I appreciate that it doesn't feel like a chain.

They're closed on Tuesdays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you actually want to impress them. It sits right on the Chattahoochee off Powers Ferry, and the view alone is worth the trip. This is date night territory, a milestone birthday, or the kind of dinner you plan ahead for.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, and the setting is intimate without feeling stuffy. It's been a go-to for special occasions around here for a long time, and it earns that reputation.

Sunday hours are split, with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so keep that in mind if you're planning a Sunday dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you need a reliable breakfast or brunch spot in Sandy Springs, J. Christopher's is the answer. It's the kind of place where you can get a solid omelet on a Tuesday morning or meet up with friends on a Saturday without a lot of fuss. Open every day but only until 2pm, so it's strictly a daytime thing.

They do classic breakfast and lunch fare well. Think omelets, burgers, and seasonal specials that rotate in throughout the year. Nothing too fancy, just good food that hits the spot.

Keep in mind they close at 2pm every day, so don't plan on a late lunch.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a tiny place over near Akers Mill that punches way above its size. There's one communal table inside and it fills up fast, so expect a line. It's worth it. This is the kind of BBQ spot that people drive across town for, and you'll understand why once you try it.

What makes it different from other BBQ places is the Korean influence mixed into the menu. It's not a gimmick, it actually works. The BBQ and burgers are what people come back for.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and they're closed Sunday and Monday. Don't show up hungry on the wrong day.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is right next door to SATE on Roswell Road and it's been a neighborhood staple for a while. It's casual and unpretentious, the kind of place you hit when you want Chinese food and don't want to think too hard about it.

The menu goes beyond the usual takeout standards. They do hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce, so there's plenty to explore if you want something a little different. Good for a weeknight dinner or grabbing takeout on the way home.

They're open late every day, until 10pm, which makes them a solid option when most other places have already shut down the kitchen.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is one of those places that punches way above its weight. The owner is genuinely passionate about what he's doing, and you can feel it from the moment you walk in. He'll talk you through the menu and explain how they make their pho the traditional way, which is a nice touch if you've never had it before.

The pho is the real draw here and reviewers say it's the real deal, not the shortcut version you get at a lot of places. It's a great pick for a quick lunch or an easy weeknight dinner, and the price is right for what you get.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes them easy to fit into your schedule.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice is a solid choice when you're in the mood for Indian food and want a place that feels welcoming even if you're not sure what to order. The staff is patient about answering questions and helping you figure out what to get, which is nice if you don't eat Indian food all the time.

Reviewers say the entrees and appetizers are both worth ordering, so don't skip the starters. It's a good spot for a sit-down dinner, and the atmosphere is quiet enough that you can actually have a conversation.

Note that they break between lunch and dinner on weekdays, closing around 2:30pm and reopening at 4:30pm. Not great for an afternoon craving, but perfect for a weeknight dinner out.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you need a solid pizza night without leaving the East Cobb area, JJ's on Roswell Road is the answer. It's casual, affordable, and the kind of place you can order from again and again without ever feeling let down. Good for a weeknight dinner, feeding the kids, or just not wanting to cook.

The stuffed pizza is what people are talking about right now. It's huge, one slice will actually fill you up, and the price makes it an easy yes. If you haven't tried it yet, that's the move.

Just keep in mind they're closed on Tuesdays. Friday and Saturday they stay open until 10, which is nice if dinner runs late.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Los Bravos is one of those spots that has been around long enough to earn a regular crowd, and the food in the kitchen is why. It's a reliable Mexican place right on Roswell Road, good for a family dinner or a casual night out when you want something filling without a lot of fuss.

The food is the draw here. Just know going in that the margaritas have gotten pricey compared to other spots nearby. A lot of regulars have noticed it. Stick with the food and you'll be happy.

They're open seven days a week and stay open late, which makes it a solid option when dinner plans come together at the last minute.
